title: Hashi
intro: Hashi (Bridges) is a logic puzzle where you connect islands with bridges.
goal: Your goal is to connect all islands with bridges so that all islands are connected and each island has the correct number of bridges.
board_example: An example board is shown below. Numbers indicate how many bridges must connect to each island.
rule_bridges: Each island (circle with a number) must have exactly as many bridges connecting to it as the number shown.
rule_direction: Bridges can only run horizontally or vertically between islands.
rule_double: Up to two bridges can connect the same pair of islands.
rule_no_cross: Bridges cannot cross each other or pass through other islands.
rule_connected: All islands must be connected together - you should be able to travel from any island to any other island via bridges.
solved: The puzzle is solved when every island has exactly the number of bridges shown, no bridges cross, and all islands are connected.
solved_board: Below is a fully solved board:
control_drag: Drag from one island to another to create bridges:
control_add: Left-drag: Add a bridge (cycles 0 to 1 to 2 to 0)
control_remove: Right-drag: Remove a bridge (cycles 2 to 1 to 0)

compact.intro: Connect islands with bridges so that:
compact.rule_count: Each island has exactly the number of bridges shown.
compact.rule_no_cross: Bridges run horizontally or vertically and cannot cross.
